name: UC Berkeley Admissions
description: Admission decisions by gender and department at UC Berkeley. Simpsons paradox. n=4,526.
studyDescription: Admission decisions for 4,526 applicants to six departments at UC Berkeley in 1973. Each row represents one applicant with their gender, department applied to, and admission decision. This is observational administrative data — applicants chose which department to apply to and were not randomly assigned. This dataset is a well-known example used to illustrate Simpson's Paradox.
sourceDetail: Bickel, P.J., Hammel, E.A., and O'Connell, J.W. (1975). "Sex Bias in Graduate Admissions: Data from Berkeley." Science, 187(4175), 398–404. Dataset from OpenIntro (ucb_admit).
